title: Condition without statement syntax error
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

```verilog
module empty ();
	parameter on = 0;
	if (on);
endmodule
```

## Expected behavior

I am not completely sure if this is actually a valid Verilog syntax, but from the spec I read the following: "if (expression) statement_or_null" so I am assuming that with the "null" part is should be legal to have a condition without a statement.

## Actual behavior

1. Executing Verilog-2005 frontend: empty.v
Parsing Verilog input from `empty.v' to AST representation.
empty.v:3: ERROR: syntax error, unexpected ';'

Sorry if this is just a question and not a bug report and thanks for the support!
